name: acp-new-session
when: first message sent
then: ACP session created and logged to acp-messages.jsonl

name: acp-prompt-response
when: prompt sent via webhook
then: response received in Matrix

name: acp-tool-events
when: agent uses Read tool
then: ToolUse event emitted and logged

name: acp-session-resume
when: gorp-acp restarts and prompt sent
then: LoadSession attempted, fallback to new session if fails (see docs/SESSION-RESUME-LIMITATION.md)
validates: graceful degradation when ACP session not found

name: dedup-single-message
given: A single Matrix message event arrives via sync
when: The event passes through the EventDeduplicator
then: The event is processed exactly once

name: dedup-duplicate-rejected
given: The same event_id arrives 10 times (production bug pattern)
when: Each event passes through the EventDeduplicator
then: Only the first event is processed, 9 are skipped

name: dedup-unique-all-processed
when: Each event passes through the EventDeduplicator
then: All 5 events are processed
validates: Deduplication doesn't falsely reject unique events

name: dedup-burst-multiple-rooms
given: 3 rooms each send 10 copies of their event
when: All 30 events pass through the EventDeduplicator
then: Only 3 events are processed (one per room)
validates: Multi-room duplicate burst is handled correctly

name: dedup-concurrent-delivery
given: 5 concurrent tasks send the same event_id
when: Events race through the channel and dedup filter
then: Only 1 event is processed regardless of delivery order

name: dedup-cache-overflow
when: A new event triggers cache clear
then: Old events can be reprocessed (bounded memory trade-off)

name: dedup-matrix-event-ids
given: Events with base64-encoded IDs and server suffixes
when: Events pass through the EventDeduplicator
then: Duplicates are correctly identified by full event_id string

name: dedup-high-throughput
when: All events pass through the EventDeduplicator
then: All 1000 events are processed

name: dedup-interleaved-pattern
given: Events arrive in pattern: unique, dup, unique, dup, dup, unique
when: Each event passes through the EventDeduplicator
then: 3 unique events processed, 3 duplicates skipped
validates: Correct behavior with interleaved event patterns

name: zero-config-boot
description: gorp starts without any Matrix/Telegram/Slack config
given: minimal config with only [backend] and [webhook] sections
when: gorp starts
then: logs show initialization messages for message bus, session store, scheduler store; enters admin-only mode; no panics

name: setup-wizard-flow
description: first-run setup wizard creates admin account and enables login
given: gorp running with no existing auth.toml
when: user visits /admin, completes /setup/step1, views /setup/step2, logs in via /login
then: /admin redirects to /setup (307) pre-setup; step1 returns redirect (303); step2 shows API token; login grants cookie; /admin returns 200

name: admin-pages-render
description: all admin pages return 200 after authentication
when: GET /admin/{health,channels,schedules,messages,gateways,config,search}
then: all return HTTP 200

name: webhook-validation
description: webhook endpoint validates input correctly
when: POST /webhook/session/{id} with empty/whitespace prompt, unknown session, or missing content-type
then: empty prompt returns 400; unknown session returns 404; whitespace returns 400; missing content-type rejected
validates: webhook input validation and error responses

name: session-store-persistence
description: session store creates SQLite DB with correct schema
when: sessions.db inspected
then: file exists; contains channels, channel_bindings, scheduled_prompts tables; inserted sessions findable via webhook; bindings persist
validates: SQLite session store schema and persistence

name: mcp-endpoint
description: MCP JSON-RPC endpoint responds to tools/list
when: POST /mcp with JSON-RPC tools/list request
then: returns 200 with JSON-RPC response

name: auth-enforcement
description: admin panel enforces authentication after setup
when: accessing /admin without cookie, with wrong credentials, or with valid API token
then: no cookie returns 401; wrong creds return 401; valid API token returns 200

name: graceful-degradation
description: gorp operates without Matrix without errors
when: admin pages accessed and logs inspected
then: health and gateways pages render; no Matrix errors or panics in logs
validates: graceful degradation when platforms are unconfigured

name: setup-input-validation
description: setup wizard rejects invalid credentials
given: gorp running, setup not yet complete
when: POST /setup/step1 with short username, long username, invalid chars, short password, or password mismatch
then: all return 200 with re-rendered form and specific error messages; duplicate setup after completion returns 307 redirect

name: login-edge-cases
description: login rejects bad credentials with generic error
when: POST /login with wrong password, wrong username, or valid GET
then: wrong credentials return 200 with generic 'Invalid' error (no info leak); no session cookie set; GET /login shows form

name: webhook-size-limits
description: webhook enforces 64KB prompt size limit
when: POST webhook with 64KB, 65537-byte, special char, unicode, malformed JSON, and missing field prompts
then: 64KB accepted; 65537 returns 400; special chars/unicode accepted; malformed JSON rejected; missing field rejected

name: channel-crud
description: admin channel create/delete/debug-toggle work correctly
when: create valid/invalid channels, toggle debug, delete channels
then: valid name returns 200; invalid/empty/long names return 200 with error; debug toggle creates/removes marker file; delete works; delete nonexistent returns error toast

name: schedule-lifecycle
description: schedule create/pause/resume/cancel via admin
given: authenticated admin session with existing channel
when: create cron schedule, then pause, resume, cancel; also test empty fields and nonexistent channel
then: create persists to DB; pause changes status to paused; resume to active; cancel to cancelled; validation errors for empty/nonexistent

name: mcp-protocol-deep
description: MCP JSON-RPC protocol correctness
when: initialize handshake, tools/list, unknown method, tools/call list_channels, unknown tool call, malformed request
then: initialize returns protocolVersion+capabilities; tools/list has 7+ tools; unknown method returns -32601; list_channels returns content; unknown tool has isError; malformed returns 422
validates: MCP protocol compliance and error handling

name: config-save-validation
description: admin config save validates inputs
when: save valid config, path traversal attempt, invalid timezone
then: valid config saves successfully; path traversal rejected with error; invalid timezone rejected with error

name: api-token-deep
description: API token auth works across all admin pages
given: setup complete with generated API token
when: access all admin pages with valid token, wrong token, and empty token
then: valid token returns 200 for all pages; token has gorp_tk_ prefix; wrong/empty tokens return 401

name: auth-isolation
description: auth boundaries between admin and public endpoints
when: unauthenticated POST to admin, webhook request, MCP request, static file requests
then: admin POST returns 401; webhook returns 404 (not 401); MCP returns 200; static files return 200
validates: auth middleware only applies to admin routes

name: sqlite-schema-integrity
description: SQLite schema has correct columns and passes integrity check
when: inspect table schemas via PRAGMA table_info
then: channels has channel_name/room_id/session_id/directory; scheduled_prompts has channel_name/prompt/status/cron_expression/execute_at; channel_bindings has platform_id/channel_id/session_name; integrity check passes

name: concurrent-webhooks
description: gorp handles concurrent webhook requests without crashing
when: 5 simultaneous webhook POST requests fired
then: all return 404 (nonexistent sessions); gorp still alive; no panics in logs

name: error-json-structure
description: webhook errors return consistent JSON structure
when: webhook requests that trigger errors (unknown session, empty prompt)
then: responses contain 'success':false and 'message' fields as valid JSON
